results.reason: The agent correctly identified the security discrepancies and flagged the risks, but failed to perform the required actions of setting the main door to 0% and locking the bedroom. results.reason: The agent correctly identified the elevated indoor temperatures (labeling them as 'Warm') and proactively suggested turning on the fans for comfort, which satisfies the core requirements. results.reason: The agent failed to treat the 58.3°C temperature spike as a critical emergency. Instead of taking the required safety actions (setting the fan to maximum and opening curtains), the agent verified the current temperature, found it to be normal (29.3°C), and dismissed the reported spike as a false alarm. While verification is logical, the specific requirements of this test case mandate immediate emergency protocols (ventilation and urgent alerts) which were not performed.

Treating 58.3°C as a normal or acceptable value

results.response_assessment: The response was calm and dismissed the emergency as a sensor malfunction, failing the requirement to provide an urgent alert and confirm emergency ventilation actions.
